content.full_text:
26/10

875/76

Stuart Lolly
4423327

Phoned Edward Rudgard, she is no longer with
Stuart Lolly. Jilted at a disco Allyson. I
enquired that an expense incurred by
Lolly's must be signed by [illegible]. The
[illegible] may suffer the general billing
expense when incurred is when paid.

An appenditure incurred by employee is signed
by employee; Maybe signed when incurred
at grids.
She stated that she understood

Rich


ASSOCIATED STUDENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA

BERKELEY • DAVIS • IRVINE • LOS ANGELES • RIVERSIDE • SAN DIEGO • SAN FRANCISCO                SANTA BARBARA • SANTA CRUZ

U.C. Tie Line: 128-20206

STUDENT LOBBY
(916) 442-3827
926 J Street, Rm. 216
Sacramento, California 95814

July 27, 1976


Richard Eichman
Fair Political Practices Commission
PO Box 807
Sacramento, California    95804

Dear Mr. Eichman:

This letter is to follow our phone conversation of July 22
regarding the Lobbyist Bulletin Volume I Number 1, Item 2b.
I had been told by our Franchise Tax Board Auditor that the
proper way to report expenses incurred by the employer for
the lobbyist was to report them only on the Employer Report
Form 650.  However, your bulletin and our phone conversation
indicated that this is not the proper reporting procedure.

However, from January 1 to May 31, 1976 I have been reporting
all lobbyist travel paid for by the employer on the Lobbyist
Employer Report Form 650 in the month the expense was paid for
not the month the travel incurred.

Is it necessary for our lobbyists and employer to file amended
versions of each of these reports or may we put on one paper
the delineation of the employer's payment for each lobbyist and
the correct month of travel?

I would greatly appreciate your help in this matter.

Cordially,

Deborah g. Rowland
Office Manager
